title: ED pregnancy bleeding documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Lists pain, amenorrhoea, bleeding, gastrointestinal symptoms, dizziness or syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, tenderness, haemodynamic signs, pregnancy testing, and escalation criteria.
red_flags.rationale: The exact NICE early-pregnancy section supports documenting onset, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, concerns, clinician risk context, measured vital data, focused assessment, appearance and time-critical symptoms without diagnosing ectopic pregnancy or miscarriage.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists pain, amenorrhoea, bleeding, gastrointestinal symptoms, dizziness or syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, tenderness, haemodynamic signs, pregnancy testing, and escalation criteria.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management

red_flags.text: Requires symptoms to take priority over hCG, limits hCG interpretation to appropriate clinical use, and requires clear worsening-symptom access and follow-up.
red_flags.rationale: The exact hCG and safety section supports documenting clinician-decided escalation, specialist discussion and arranged handover or follow-up, without setting thresholds or intervals.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Requires symptoms to take priority over hCG, limits hCG interpretation to appropriate clinical use, and requires clear worsening-symptom access and follow-up.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.8.1–1.8.10 — hCG in pregnancy of unknown location

investigations.text: Defines clinician-performed transvaginal or selected transabdominal ultrasound and cautions against overinterpreting one early scan or LMP alone.
investigations.rationale: The exact ultrasound section supports recording existing tests and imaging already performed or reviewed, without recommending or interpreting them.
investigations.evidence_extract: Defines clinician-performed transvaginal or selected transabdominal ultrasound and cautions against overinterpreting one early scan or LMP alone.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.7.6 — ultrasound for pregnancy location and viability

referral.text: Defines the up-to-13-week complication population and services offering specialist assessment, ultrasound, serum hCG review, and timely referral.
referral.rationale: The exact service section supports a clinician-entered plan and completed risk or consent discussion only when documented; it does not create management.
referral.evidence_extract: Defines the up-to-13-week complication population and services offering specialist assessment, ultrasound, serum hCG review, and timely referral.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management

exact_section.heading: Overview and recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.4 — early pregnancy assessment services

source_limitations: Gestational age, last menstrual period, bleeding amount, tissue, pain detail, syncope, obstetric history, Rh status, pelvic examination, test values, diagnosis, treatment and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
